ecosystem: Coastal edge Pacific Northwest (tidal flat, estuary, adjacent forest, open water)
family: Multi-species multi-taxa (birds, amphibians, insects, marine mammals)

acoustic_nodes.description: Low register (1-3 kHz): Swainson's Thrush upward-spiraling song, dawn chorus anchor; Hermit Thrush two-syrinx polyphony. Mid register (2-5 kHz): Wilson's/Yellow/Orange-crowned Warblers coastal spring migrants, dense simultaneous texture. High register (4-8+ kHz): Pacific Wren complex rapid trill. Marsh low register (1-4 kHz): Red-winged Blackbird 'konk-la-ree' dominant marsh acoustic signature. 15-30 species simultaneously at peak.

Pacific Tree Frog (Pseudacris regilla) 2-4 kHz peak 3.2 kHz. 'Ribbit' -- Hollywood generic frog sound; this PNW species is the de facto global representation of frog sound. 60 calls/minute breeding season. Continuous chorus from pre-midnight through dawn; maximum spring breeding intensity March-May. Sustained high-rate background against which bird songs are superimposed. Frog continuous; birds structured.

acoustic_nodes.layer: Marine mammal layer -- below-threshold frequency extension
acoustic_nodes.description: Harbor Seal above-water vocalizations 100-8,000 Hz including breeding calls, threat, contact. Coastal haul-out sites tidal flats Salish Sea. Low-frequency register extends full spectrum downward: frogs+birds (1-8+ kHz) + seals (0.1-1.5 kHz) = full audible spectrum 100 Hz to 15+ kHz simultaneously at coastal edge location.
acoustic_nodes.energy_contribution: Low-frequency extension layer, full spectrum completion

acoustic_nodes.description: Spring cricket katydid stridulation 3-15 kHz. Pacific Northwest insect acoustics spring-minimal. Late April-May adds high-frequency texture at upper edge. Near-audible 12-20 kHz extends acoustic environment toward ultrasonic. Bats foraging add 20-100 kHz if present.

theory_nodes.concept: Dawn chorus timing structure: species-specific sequence following light curve and visual acuity thresholds. Earliest (thrushes, some sparrows) begin 60-90 minutes before sunrise. Maximum density at civil twilight (6 degrees below horizon) -- most species simultaneously active before foraging competition begins. Convergence principle: maximum = most species simultaneous, not loudest single moment.
theory_nodes.description: Dawn chorus timing -- species sequence, civil twilight maximum, convergence not loudness.

theory_nodes.concept: Coastal edge location as enabling condition: tidal flat + estuary marsh + riparian forest + open water = maximum acoustic layer co-presence. Marine mammal voices (harbor seal) possible only at coast; frog chorus from marsh; bird layer from forest and marsh edge; insect layer from marsh vegetation. The coastal edge is the geographic location where all four trophic layers are simultaneously within range.
theory_nodes.description: Coastal edge as enabling condition -- four trophic layers geographically co-present.

theory_nodes.concept: Full-spectrum coverage as E=10 indicator: combined acoustic profile frogs (2-4 kHz) + birds (1-8+ kHz) + insects (3-15 kHz) + harbor seals (0.1-1.5 kHz) covers full audible frequency range 100 Hz to 15+ kHz simultaneously. Comparable to degree 351 (Hoh Rainforest Insect+Bird+Rain, full spectrum achieved through rainforest mechanism). All-species coastal dawn achieves same through marine mammal addition.
theory_nodes.description: Full spectrum coverage -- 100 Hz to 15+ kHz, marine mammal enables low-frequency, coastal enables convergence.

theory_nodes.concept: E=10 convergence calibration: spring (maximum breeding all species simultaneously) + coastal edge (maximum layer co-presence) + dawn (temporal bird chorus maximum) = three conditions at simultaneous peak. Individual components: bird dawn chorus E=7-8 alone; frog chorus E=5-6; harbor seal E=4-5. Intersection of all at simultaneous peak = E=10. Convergence is the event.
theory_nodes.description: E=10 convergence -- three conditions simultaneous peak, components E=5-8 alone, intersection E=10.

cross_references.relationship: Both achieve full-spectrum acoustic coverage. 351 (Hoh): insect + bird + rain in rainforest, specific old-growth mechanism. 358 (coastal edge): birds + frogs + insects + marine mammals, coastal mechanism. Two routes to full-spectrum coverage in the same engine.

Multi-species nocturnal chorus (353) vs multi-species diurnal dawn chorus (358). 353 = temporal development model, winter, E=9. 358 = convergence maximum model, spring, E=10. Same multi-species principle, different temporal structure and energy calibration.

E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Simultaneous voices in distinct frequency registers creating whole exceeding parts. Sleater-Kinney three voices (Brownstein/Tucker/Weiss) in distinct registers. All-species dawn chorus four trophic layers in distinct frequency registers. Architectural convergence pairing.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Simultaneous distinct voices in distinct registers. Sleater-Kinney: three voices, three frequency register zones, architectural interplay creating whole exceeding parts. All-species dawn chorus: four trophic layers, four frequency register zones (0.1-1.5 kHz seals, 1-8+ kHz birds, 2-4 kHz frogs, 3-15 kHz insects), convergence creating whole exceeding parts.
